_scope.storage_model: In-memory Map-of-Map stores (repoStore, snapshotStore, scoreStore, reasonStore, categoryStore, mentionStore) hydrated on boot from JSONL files under src/lib/pipeline/storage. No real indexes. Every query is a linear scan of an array returned by Map.values().
_scope.current_scale: Seed list ~300 repos (src/lib/seed-repos.ts). snapshotStore has per-repo cap SNAPSHOT_HISTORY_CAP=120 (~30d @ 6h cadence). Deltas are precomputed per recompute and baked onto Repo rows, so sort-by-delta is already O(1) read.

filters.complexity: O(N) scan over page repos; reads precomputed r.movementStatus
filters.latency_x100: ~5ms @ 30k (but UI pipeline not designed to ship 30k rows over the wire)
edge_cases: movementStatus is recomputed on every recomputeAll; stale after a pipeline crash between ingest and recompute
Setting any meta filter forces activeTab='trending' via store — tab selection silently lost
bugs_or_smells: UI-only filter: the API route /api/repos also exposes filter=hot but the FilterBar/MetasBar applies client-side. Two code paths (applyMetaFilter + applyLocalFilter + pipeline.applyTrendFilter) duplicate the same logic in 3 files — drift risk.

edge_cases: movementStatus may be 'stable' until next recompute
bugs_or_smells: Same as hot: duplicated in /api/repos applyLocalFilter AND queries/service applyTrendFilter — 3 copies.
Same 3-way duplication

edge_cases: cutoff = Date.now() - 30*86_400_000; re-evaluated every render ⇒ unstable useMemo dep when Date.now drifts (but it's inside useMemo without Date.now in deps, so it only re-runs on other dep change — filter window frozen to last dep change)
Repos with unparseable createdAt silently pass the cutoff test via NaN < cutoff → false, correct but not defensive
bugs_or_smells: 30d magic number lives in 3 places: filters.ts:31, queries/service.ts:66, /api/repos:63 — change one and only one path updates.
Date.parse called on every pass; could be precomputed once onto Repo.

edge_cases: mentionCount24h is a 24h rolling count but recomputed only when recomputeAll runs — NOT actually 24h rolling at query time
bugs_or_smells: Silently lies: 'Most Discussed (24h)' is a stale snapshot; if recompute hasn't run in 12h the '24h' label is misleading.

edge_cases: Placeholder: code comment admits previousRank isn't tracked; just returns rank 1-20 = 'climbers'. Wholly fake signal at the UI layer.
meta-counts.ts/getRankClimbers has a real reason-backed path but applyMetaFilter does NOT use it — UI filter and UI count disagree.
bugs_or_smells: SILENT LIE: top-20 by rank is NOT 'climbers'. A repo ranked #1 forever still qualifies.

Two different semantics: meta-counts getRankClimbers (reason.codes.includes('rank_jump')) vs applyMetaFilter (rank<=20). Count badge in MetasBar shows one number, clicking the pill filters by the other.

edge_cases: 14-day cutoff in UI but meta-counts uses 48h + fallback 7d — THREE separate release windows
bugs_or_smells: SILENT LIE: label says 'Fresh Releases' but count (48h or 7d fallback), filter (14d), and query API /api/pipeline/featured/getFreshReleases (hoursBack param) all use different cutoffs.

filters.applied_at: hybrid (client filter in useFilteredRepos; server filter in /api/repos?tag=)
filters.complexity: O(N * T) where T = avg tags per repo
edge_cases: Client uses r.tags.includes(activeTag) exact match; server regex-validates tag against /^[a-z0-9-]+$/ — client does NOT validate, so a malformed store value would silently match nothing
Tag derivation (deriveTags) runs inside recomputeAll only; tags are stale between recomputes
bugs_or_smells: Tag applied CLIENT side against whatever repos[] the page fetched. /api/repos?tag= is never actually called from the MetasBar/TagsBar components — TagsBar toggles activeTag in the store, useFilteredRepos filters in-memory. The server tag path exists but is orphaned.
Case sensitivity: tag ids are lowercased by convention but no normalization at the store boundary. A hand-set activeTag='Claude-Code' would match nothing.

edge_cases: category is a string id but NOT validated against CATEGORIES list anywhere in useFilteredRepos — a stale URL with unknown category filters to zero results silently
Repo.categoryId is set in recomputeAll from classification.primary.categoryId; between ingest and first recompute new repos have the ingest-time placeholder
bugs_or_smells: Inconsistent scope: /categories/[slug] uses pipeline.getReposByCategory (repoStore.getAll, NOT getActive) — surfaces deleted/archived tombstones by default, relying on the downstream excludeArchived toggle to hide them.
Server category filter in /api/repos uses getCategoryMovers which reads repoStore.getAll(), while global path uses repoStore.getActive() — category pages leak archived/deleted.

filters.complexity: O(N * L) where L = selected languages
edge_cases: languages.includes(r.language) is EXACT case-sensitive match — GitHub returns 'TypeScript' with exact capitalization so matches, but a user-entered list would silently fail
Repos where r.language === null always fail (correct; excluded from language filter)
bugs_or_smells: Case sensitivity: no toLowerCase anywhere. If the sidebar ever accepted free-text entry this breaks.
Persisted in localStorage (partialize includes 'languages') but if the source repo dataset drops a language, stale-selected language silently hides every row.

edge_cases: range is [min, max] INCLUSIVE on both sides — stars==min passes, stars==max passes. Off-by-one ambiguity if UI copy says 'under 1000'.
null range = everything passes (correct).
No validation that min <= max; if store has [5000, 100] everything filters out.
bugs_or_smells: SILENT LIE POTENTIAL: filter applies to current absolute stars, NOT to delta. A sidebar labelled 'min_stars: 1000' cannot filter by delta; there is no delta-range filter anywhere.
Not persisted (starsRange absent from partialize) — contradicts languages/onlyWatched persistence.

edge_cases: minMomentum=0 (default) skips the filter entirely (correct).
Negative values never produced by store API but not guarded.
bugs_or_smells: Not persisted (absent from partialize) but 'languages' is — inconsistent persistence policy.
momentumScore is recomputed in recomputeAll; reading mid-recompute could show fresh scores paired with stale rank, but recompute is synchronous so minor risk.

edge_cases: Set is built inside useMemo — rebuilt only when watchedItems or source changes. Correct.

Watchlist IDs are slug-normalized by the store (addRepo takes repoId as-is); a watchlist entry with a non-slug id (e.g. 'vercel/next.js') will NEVER match r.id which is always 'vercel--next-js'. No normalization at add time.
bugs_or_smells: Mismatch with /api/repos?ids=… path which DOES normalize via slugToId (route.ts:94). Store path does not. Adding a repo from a legacy URL that reached store.addRepo("vercel/next.js", …) would leak into localStorage and be un-matchable until manually removed.

edge_cases: Applies LAST in the filter chain (after meta + tag) — correct conceptually but means the meta/tag counts on MetasBar might include archived repos while the table excludes them. Badge numbers lie.
Default is TRUE so archived/deleted never surface unless the user toggles.
bugs_or_smells: r.archived?:boolean — undefined is treated as not-archived (r.archived !== true). OK. Same for r.deleted.

filters.complexity: O(N * (1 + |desc| + |topics|)) — toLowerCase called PER REPO PER QUERY, no normalized column cached
filters.latency_x100: ~250-500ms @ 30k — FIRST TO BLOW UP because toLowerCase creates strings allocs each call
edge_cases: Empty query returns [] (short-circuit at service.ts:291).
Query is lowercased but substring match, not token match — 'type script' won't match 'typescript'.
Topics loop allocates per-topic lowercase string every call.
bugs_or_smells: NO fuzzy match, NO typo tolerance, NO scoring by match quality — always sorted by momentumScore desc.
repoStore.getActive() excludes deleted but NOT archived — a recently-archived repo still shows in search.
Category-scoped search uses pipeline.searchReposByQuery with categoryId option, but the route-level category param is passed through; no validation of category against CATEGORIES.

edge_cases: under-1k-stars hardcoded 1000. 'new-under-30d' hardcoded 30d. Not configurable.
Strict validation: invalid filter returns 400 (good).
category+filter composed: pipeline.getCategoryMovers(1000 cap) then local applyTrendFilter — double-scan but on ≤1000 rows.
bugs_or_smells: getCategoryMovers reads repoStore.getAll() (includes tombstones); global path uses getActive. Inconsistent between category-scoped and global filter paths.

filters.complexity: O(N log N) — sorts the full set
edge_cases: Reads precomputed starsDelta24h/7d/30d — these are baked in recomputeAll. If recompute is delayed, 'today' delta can be up to 6h stale (cron cadence).
No 'all-time' window.
sort=newest uses new Date(createdAt).getTime() — invalid date → NaN, NaN subtraction moves the row unpredictably in sort (non-deterministic order).
sort is applied AFTER applyTrendFilter, so paginating 'stars-today' with filter='breakouts' is consistent as long as the pipeline state doesn't mutate mid-request.
bugs_or_smells: sortResults/sortRepos/sortReposByColumn — THREE sort implementations that overlap (search, repos, filters.ts). Delta-normalized sort (delta24h/stars) only exists in filters.ts, not server routes. UI sort and API sort disagree on 'stars-today' semantics (client: delta/stars ratio, server: raw delta).

edge_cases: Empty tag string (after trim) returns 400.
Regex ^[a-z0-9-]+$ rejects uppercase, underscores, dots — consistent with deriveTags output.
bugs_or_smells: This server-side tag filter is NOT reached by the normal UI path — TagsBar uses the client-only activeTag store filter. Orphaned endpoint.

edge_cases: Delta columns (delta24h/7d/30d) normalize by current stars via Math.max(r.stars,1) — protects div-zero but a 10-star repo with +5 delta outranks a 10k repo with +1000 on 'delta24h' sort.
'chart' column sorts by raw starsDelta7d (NOT normalized) — inconsistent with delta7d column.
string localeCompare branch used when extractor returns string; mixing number/string values within a column silently coerces.
'actions' column extractor returns () => 0 — sorting by actions is a no-op but allowed by the type.
bugs_or_smells: Client sort runs AFTER server sort — two sort passes with potentially different keys for the same listing.
Direction is applied consistently but default is 'desc' for every column via store presets — sort on 'repo' (string) defaults to reverse alphabetical, which is surprising UX.

filters.applied_at: client (sort preset, not a true filter)
edge_cases: Setting activeTab clears activeMetaFilter (destructive). Setting activeMetaFilter force-sets activeTab='trending'. They cannot coexist.
Tab 'new' preset sorts by lastCommit desc — NOT by createdAt. So 'New' tab is really 'most-recent commit' not 'recently created'.
Tab 'watchlisted' preset sorts by momentum but does NOT filter to watched repos — the filter has to come from elsewhere (onlyWatched toggle or /watchlist page source).
bugs_or_smells: SILENT LIE: 'New' tab ≠ new repos. User clicks 'New' expecting recently-created projects, actually gets recently-active projects.
'Watchlisted' tab is a pure sort preset; on home page it does NOT narrow to the user's watchlist. Works only because /watchlist passes a pre-filtered source.

filters.applied_at: client (store only; never wired to API)
edge_cases: Set via TimeRangePills but no consumer actually filters by timeRange — the delta columns are all shown side-by-side. Store value is effectively a breadcrumb.
bugs_or_smells: DEAD FILTER: timeRange is set in store, has an action, but nothing reads it to filter/sort. It's not in the useFilteredRepos dep list. Ship as dead code or wire up.

combined_paths.path: search page: q + category + sort (server) then client sidebar filters (category/languages/starsRange/minMomentum/onlyWatched/meta/tag/excludeArchived)
combined_paths.composition: Server narrows by q + category (substring scan), returns up to 50, client then reapplies FULL sidebar filter pipeline inside useFilteredRepos. Category is reapplied on client even though server already applied it — harmless dup-check.
combined_paths.cost: server: O(N) scan of repoStore.getActive(); client: O(M) with M ≤ 50
issues: Client applies category AGAIN on the already-category-filtered server result — no harm, but if URL category differs from store category, the store wins and silently hides the server results. URL vs store drift.

combined_paths.path: home page: pipeline.getTopMovers('today', 80) → client filters + client sort
combined_paths.composition: Server returns top 80 only; any client filter narrows further. If the user enables a restrictive filter (e.g. category=crypto-web3 + minMomentum=80), the result is 'top 80 today' ∩ crypto — which is NOT the top-80 crypto today. Two layers of ranking don't compose.
issues: SILENT LIE: sidebar category filter on the home page does NOT re-query to that category's actual top movers. A crypto repo ranked #120 globally never appears even though it's the #1 crypto mover.

combined_paths.path: category detail page: pipeline.getReposByCategory(slug) [ALL repos in cat, no limit] → client filters + client sort
combined_paths.composition: Full category slice (uses repoStore.getAll, no active filter) goes to client.
issues: Surfaces archived/deleted repos by default because getReposByCategory uses getAll(). Only hidden by excludeArchived client toggle (default on). Turn it off → tombstones appear.

combined_paths.composition: order: pipeline.getTopMovers|getCategoryMovers (already limited to 1000) → applyLocalFilter → tag filter → sortRepos → slice(offset, offset+limit). Each step is O(N) over a capped 1000 rows.
issues: 1000 hard cap is silent: if seed grows past 1000 a category will never surface its 1001st repo via /api/repos.
getCategoryMovers uses getAll (tombstones), getTopMovers uses getActive — inconsistent base set.

combined_paths.composition: Separate code paths: meta-counts.ts walks repoStore.getActive once and returns 7 counts. applyMetaFilter walks the repos-on-page client-side and filters. They read different sources (all active vs page repos) so counts and visible rows diverge.
issues: Counts include repos the page never fetched. Home page fetches top 80 only, but MetasBar says '412 hot' — clicking hot filters 80 → subset. User perceives inconsistency.

pagination.type: offset+limit (only on /api/repos and /api/pipeline/rebuild). /api/search uses limit-only, no offset. No cursor-based pagination anywhere.
issues: /api/search has NO offset — caller can only get top-N; paging beyond N impossible (limit capped at 100).
Offset pagination over a mutable in-memory store: recomputeAll rewrites rank/momentum and sort order on every tick. A second page request after a recompute can skip or repeat repos.
No 'ETag' or 'snapshot token' — callers have no way to pin results to a pipeline version.
Home, categories, watchlist, and search UIs do NOT paginate — they slice a fixed limit (80/all/persisted/50) and trust the client to scroll. No infinite-scroll, no 'next page' UI.
Compare endpoint caps at 5 but compareStore UI caps at 4 — off-by-one between store and API.

sort_orders.invalid: TS-typed; unknown column id falls through switch and the sort is undefined behavior (extract returns undefined → NaN comparisons)

sort_orders.keys: momentum desc (fixed) except MostDiscussed=mentionCount24h desc, NewRepos=momentum desc, Search=momentum desc

seed_leak_risk: LOW at query layer, MEDIUM at ingestion layer. Every user-facing query reads repoStore which is populated exclusively from the seed list (src/lib/seed-repos.ts) via /api/cron/seed or /api/pipeline/rebuild. /api/search specifically reads repoStore.getActive() and cannot surface a non-seed repo. HOWEVER: /api/pipeline/ingest and /api/pipeline/rebuild accept arbitrary owner/name pairs in the body (not validated against the seed list), so an operator with an API key can insert non-seed repos into the store. After insertion, every query (search, categories, featured) will surface them. This is an auth/admin control, not a filter control — but it means 'search is seed-bounded' is only true by convention.

ux_smell_ranking.smell: rank-climbers meta pill: count uses reason.rank_jump, filter uses rank<=20 — badge and filtered list disagree (src/lib/filters.ts:37 vs src/lib/pipeline/queries/meta-counts.ts:139)
'New' tab sorts by lastCommit, not createdAt — label lies (src/lib/store.ts:180)
Fresh-releases uses THREE different cutoffs across UI filter (14d), count (48h), server query (variable hoursBack). Count badge, filtered list, and featured row all disagree.
Home page sidebar category filter compounds onto already-truncated top-80 — users get 'top 80 today AND crypto', not 'top crypto today' (src/app/page.tsx:24)
timeRange (24h/7d/30d) is dead — stored, toggled, never read by any filter
Three duplicate filter implementations for hot/breakouts/quiet-killers/new/under-1k (filters.ts, api/repos, queries/service). Drift inevitable.
/categories/[slug] uses repoStore.getAll (tombstones) while / uses getActive. Archived flag toggle flips whether tombstones appear ONLY on category detail page.
Watchlist id normalization mismatch: store.addRepo() accepts raw ids without slug normalization, but /api/repos?ids= does normalize. Legacy localStorage entries can be un-matchable.
Server-side /api/repos?tag= endpoint is orphaned — UI applies tag filter client-side, so pagination over tag filter never works (all-in-page).
starsRange inclusive on both ends with no UI affordance for min>max — empty result if swapped.
sort direction defaults to desc for every column, including 'repo' (fullName string) — reverse alphabetical surprising.
sortColumn='chart' sorts by raw starsDelta7d, but sortColumn='delta7d' sorts by starsDelta7d/stars. Same-looking column, different semantics.
minMomentum, starsRange not in partialize() — behave differently from languages/onlyWatched on reload.
Offset pagination over recompute-mutable store can skip/repeat rows across pages — no cursor.
Featured cards limit param differs from home default (page passes 8, URL may pass 1-20) but no effect on other surfaces.
MetasBar counts come from /api/pipeline/meta-counts (total active repos) but applyMetaFilter only filters the currently-fetched repos[] — badge often shows more than visible.
